title: Research: file-reference notation for inlining and sub-tree extraction
description: Design a syntax for nota to reference external files so that (a) a file's contents can be inlined as a string value, and (b) a sub-tree of a record can be extracted into a separate file and referenced. Goal: avoid ugly deeply-recursive data in single files. Research first — survey YAML !include, Jsonnet import, Dhall ./relative.dhall, HCL file(), terraform templatefile, Nix builtins.readFile, JSON-Schema $ref. Cover: path resolution (relative to what?), cycle detection, canonical-form implications (does a content-addressed hash include referenced files transitively?), streaming vs eager load, security (can a user arbitrarily exfiltrate files?). Deferred until after the positional-records rewrite ships. From mentci-next report 008 §2.2.

title: File-reference notation — research after syntax re-stabilizes
description: Li wants nota to support (a) referring to a file whose contents become a string value, and (b) splitting inner parts of a document across multiple files to avoid ugly deeply-recursive single-file data. Research survey before designing: YAML !include, Jsonnet import, Dhall ./relative.dhall, HCL file(), TOML (no native), XML entities/xinclude. Decide: syntax shape, cycle detection, security model (sandbox paths?), relative-path resolution base, canonical-form implications, streaming vs eager load. Deferred until field-separator (=) decision and related syntax changes land — don't churn the format before resolving the bigger shape decisions.
